MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SMARDAN SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Patrick Campbell of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Michael Sachs of Adams Nye Becht LLP (415.982.8955) for defendant and moving party.

Argued. The oral request for additional briefing is denied. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Smardan Supply Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether decedent Carl Pintur was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits J, L, M and N (Mr. Marquez's deposition transcripts and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration) are sustained and the remainder of defendant's objections are overruled. Plaintiffs withdrew Sam Marquez as a witness against defendant. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. MINI MINUTES: **** Ruling on Plaintiff's Motion for Preference - continued ****

No later than March 2, 2016 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than March 2, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on April 20, 2016 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on June 13, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Appearances:
Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and moving party; Constance Fraenkel of Spanos Przetak (designated defense counsel); Gina Haran of Adams Nye for defendant Smardan; Jeff Thayer of DeHay for defendant Kaiser Gypsum; Thomas Steig of Prindle for defendant Trane US, Inc. ; Jay Picking of Pray Price Williams & Picking (562.436.1231) appearing via Court Call for defendant Bragg Investment Company, Inc.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to CCP 36(a). Trial date is June 13, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 2, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the CCP, unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Plaintiffs' depositions must conclude no later than March 17, 2016, unless parties stipulate otherwise. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the CCP, whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 8, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than April 22, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by June 6, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on June 9, 2016. On agreement of counsel, Fact discovery and Expert discovery shall remain open until the case is assigned for trial. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 11, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. Counsel are directed to meet and confer on the language regarding submitting additional documents to a bankruptcy trust.
